scope: Supplemental pass — hotel tax changes 2024-2025 for destinations NOT covered by the primary 2026-04-22 file. Focuses on Asia-Pacific, Caribbean, Americas (esp. US major cities), Nordics, Eastern Europe, and additional Italian/Portuguese cities.
methodology: 20+ WebSearch queries targeting specific destinations and thematic 2024-2025 tax changes. Each finding has at least one cited source.
summary: 21 additional findings beyond the primary file. Highlights: Hong Kong resumed 3% HAT (Jan 2025, was 0% since 2008); India slashed GST on budget hotels 12%→5% (Sept 2025); Melbourne Short Stay Levy 7.5% (Jan 2025); Chicago 17.4% (highest in US); Norway approved 3% tourist tax for select cities (summer 2026); Seychelles new sustainability levy (Jan 2026); Baja California Sur $25 entry tax (Jul 2025); Italian Jubilee-year hikes in Milan/Florence. Plus several stable-rate entries for completeness.

findings.summary: Hotel Accommodation Tax (HAT) RESUMED at 3% on Jan 1, 2025, after 17 years at 0% (was waived in 2008 during financial crisis). Expected to generate HK$1.1 billion annually.

rates.source_quote: With effect from 1 January 2025, the HAT rate will be adjusted from 0% to 3%.
rates.legal_reference: Hong Kong Legislative Council resolution Oct 23, 2024; gazette Oct 25, 2024

findings.summary: Victoria Short Stay Levy Act 2024: 7.5% levy on short-term accommodation (<27 consecutive nights) bookings, effective Jan 1, 2025. Collected by the platform.

rates.source_quote: The Short Stay Levy Act 2024 (Vic) introduced a 7.5% levy on short-term accommodation bookings, effective 1 January 2025.

rules.name: 27-night threshold
rules.description: Levy applies only to stays shorter than 27 consecutive days

findings.summary: ACT short-term rental levy at 5%, effective July 1, 2025. Mirrors the Victoria model at a lower rate.

rates.source_quote: A similar short-term rental accommodation levy applies at a rate of 5% in the Australian Capital Territory from 1 July 2025.

findings.summary: MAJOR GST reform Sept 22, 2025: hotel rooms ≤₹7,500/night GST cut from 12% to 5% (without ITC). Rooms >₹7,500/night remain at 18% (with ITC). Substantial reduction for budget/mid-range hotels.

rates.source_quote: Starting September 22, 2025, hotel rooms priced below ₹7500 per night now attract only 5% GST, down from the previous 12% rate.
rates.legal_reference: GST Council notification; CBIC circular Sept 2025

rates.source_quote: Rooms priced above Rs.7,500 will continue to attract 18% GST, and ITC is fully available here.

rules.name: ₹7500 threshold — reduced GST
rules.description: Hotel rooms priced ≤₹7,500/night: 5% GST (reduced from 12% on 2025-09-22)

sources.title: GST Rate Changes for Hotels Sept 22, 2025

findings.notes: Nightly rate threshold is in INR. Rate value stored in local currency — rule engine would need currency-aware comparison.

findings.summary: Milan tourist tax raised to €7/person/night for some accommodations from Jan 1, 2025 — Jubilee Year 2025 increase authorized by national government (up to €2 extra per person per night). Reverts to 2024 rates on Jan 1, 2026.

rates.source_quote: from January 1st, 2025 the tax will be €7.00 per person per night for certain accommodation types.

rules.name: Jubilee-year surcharge window
rules.description: Additional €2/person/night authorized for 2025 only; reverts to 2024 rates Jan 1, 2026

findings.summary: Florence tourist tax ranges €4.50-€8/person/night in 2025 (Jubilee-year increase). Airbnb/B&B capped at €5.50/night from August 2025. 7-night max.

rates.source_quote: In 2025, the city's tourist taxes will range from €4 to €8 per night... Airbnb and B&B stays are capped at €5.50 per night as of August 2025.

rules.name: 7-night cap
rules.description: Tax payable for up to 7 consecutive nights

findings.jurisdiction_name: Baja California Sur (Los Cabos, La Paz)
findings.summary: 'Embrace It' tax launched July 1, 2025. 470 pesos (since raised to 488, ~$25-28 USD) per international visitor age 12+. Applies if staying >24 hours. Prepaid online with QR code. Previously voluntary, now mandatory.

rates.authority_name: Gobierno del Estado de Baja California Sur
rates.source_quote: On July 1, 2025, the government of Baja California Sur launched an 'Embrace It' tourist fee of 470 pesos (since raised to 488 pesos, or about US $28), mandatory for everyone over the age of 12 who arrives in the state by land or air from outside Mexico, and stays for more than 24 hours.

rules.name: 24-hour minimum stay
rules.description: Applies only if stay exceeds 24 hours

findings.summary: Tourism Enhancement Fee USD $20 per international visitor. Landing tax $20, departure tax $35 (for air arrivals). Stable rates; Jamaica cracking down on Airbnb tax collection.
Cruise passenger departure tax raised from $18 to $23 in 2024. Plus $5 environmental tourism tax + $2 enhancement tax.

rates.source_quote: In 2024, The Bahamas overhauled its cruise tax system, with passengers departing from Nassau and Freeport now paying an increased departure tax of $23, up from $18, plus a $5 environmental tourism tax and a $2 enhancement tax.
rates.notes: Cruise-only. Does not apply to hotel stays for flight arrivals.

findings.summary: Tourism Environmental Sustainability Levy (updated regulations) effective Jan 1, 2026. SCR 75/person/night medium accommodations (25-50 rooms), SCR 100 large (50+ rooms). Small (<25 rooms) now exempt. Residents/airline/yacht crew/children ≤12 exempt.

rates.source_quote: From January 1, 2026, the levy will be charged per person per night at SCR 75 for medium accommodation establishments (25–50 rooms) and SCR 100 for large accommodation establishments (more than 50 rooms).

findings.summary: Porto tourist tax raised to €3/person/night in 2024 (was €2). Capped at 7 consecutive nights (max €21). Year-round rate. Revenue up 42% in early 2025.

rates.source_quote: Porto increased its tourist tax in 2024 to €3 per person per night, year-round.

findings.summary: Funchal tourist tax €2/person/night, capped at 7 nights. NEW 2025: €2/cruise passenger disembarking at Funchal port.

Several Algarve municipalities introduced seasonal tourist tax. Albufeira and Lagoa-Carvoeiro-Ferragudo charge €2/night peak, €1/night off-peak. 7-night cap. Only 7 of 16 Algarve municipalities currently levy.

rates.notes: Highly variable by municipality; only ~7 of 16 Algarve towns collect as of 2025.

findings.summary: Legislation approved 2025 — select Norwegian municipalities can impose 3% tourist tax on overnight stays and cruise passengers. Opt-in basis (Bergen, Tromsø, possibly Oslo first). Targeted for summer 2026.

rates.authority_name: Kingdom of Norway (enabling legislation; local municipalities implement)
rates.source_quote: Select municipalities in Norway can impose a 3% fee on overnight stays under newly approved legislation.

findings.summary: Budapest tourist tax: 4% of room rate + ~1000 HUF/person/night (~€2.48). Under-18 exempt.
Local hotel tax (Taxa Hoteliera Locala): 1% of accommodation cost per night. In tourist resorts, only first night is charged. Under-18 exempt.

rates.authority_name: Local municipalities under Romanian Tax Code

rules.name: Resort first-night-only
rules.description: In tourist resorts, tax is charged only for the first night of stay

findings.summary: Melaka Heritage Tax increased Jan 1, 2025: <3 star RM 2→3, 3-4 star RM 2→4, 5 star new RM 5 per room per night. On top of federal Tourism Tax (RM 10/night) and SST.

rates.source_quote: properties below three stars seeing an increase from RM2.00 to RM3.00 per room per night, while three to four-star hotels increased from RM2.00 to RM4.00, and five-star hotels increased to RM5.00.

findings.summary: Federal Tourism Tax (TTx): RM 10/room/night (non-residents only; Malaysians/PR exempt). Plus 8% SST on accommodation services (if annual turnover >RM 500k).

rules.name: Malaysian nationals/PR exempt
rules.description: Tourism Tax applies to non-residents only

findings.summary: Chicago hotel tax now 17.4% after council vote — highest in US. Stack: 6% state + 2% Illinois Sports Facilities Authority + 2.5% Metropolitan Pier & Exposition Authority + 1% municipal + city/county overlays.

rates.authority_name: City of Chicago (stack of state + city + special-purpose taxes)
rates.source_quote: In Chicago, the total hotel tax can reach 17.4%, which is a recent development, as the city now has the highest hotel tax in the U.S. after a council vote.
rates.notes: Combined stack — need to decompose into component rates for proper modeling.

findings.summary: Boston room occupancy excise: 6.5% local + 2.75% convention center funding + 5.7% state = ~14.95% total.

rates.source_quote: Boston's local room occupancy excise was increased from 6% to 6.5%.

findings.summary: Aruba Sustainability Fee $20/person (air arrivals age 8+) via ED card system from July 2024. Plus existing room-night tax.

rates.source_quote: Since July 2024, Aruba has required air travelers aged 8 and older to pay a $20 sustainability fee, collected through the Embarkation and Disembarkation (ED) card system.

findings.summary: Vietnam VAT on accommodation remains reduced to 8% (from standard 10%) through 30 June 2025. Hotels add 10% government tax + ~5% service charge. No tourist entry tax.

rates.source_quote: The National Assembly of Vietnam recently approved to extend the policy of 2% Value Added Tax (VAT) rate reduction to 30 June 2025.

findings.summary: Costa Rica 13% VAT (IVA) on all tourism services since July 1, 2023. Plus $15 arrival tax included in airfare. Stable into 2026.

rates.authority_name: Ministerio de Hacienda de Costa Rica

proposals_not_yet_enacted.status: City council voted 32-20 late 2025 to propose; industry analysts estimate >50% probability of passage in 2026
Planned for 2025 to fund aviation greening

proposals_not_yet_enacted.change: HB 298 — +1% hotel tax + STR max cap 6.75% → 7.75%
proposals_not_yet_enacted.status: Introduced April 2025; requires governing-authority and electorate approval
Announced for July 1, 2025; may still be in deferral/phased implementation
Announced Nov 2025; effective Jan 1, 2026 — not yet detailed

observations: 2024-2025 was the biggest two-year window for new accommodation tax launches since the VAT rollout era. Hong Kong, Melbourne, ACT, Baja California Sur, Aruba, Porto, Bahamas cruise, Norway framework, Edinburgh (first in Scotland), and multiple Japanese prefectures all launched new or substantially raised taxes.
India is the counter-trend: GST on budget hotels cut in half (12% → 5%), making India one of the few places actively REDUCING accommodation tax to support tourism.
Italian Jubilee-year hikes (Milan, Florence, Rome et al.) are one-year bumps — reverting Jan 1, 2026. Important for temporal modeling.
Caribbean is consolidating toward sustainability-fee model (Aruba, Bahamas, Seychelles, Baja California Sur) vs. percentage room taxes.
Chicago now holds the US crown at 17.4% total, overtaking NYC (~14.75%) and LA/SF (14%).
Common 2024-2025 pattern: Under-12 / Under-18 exemption; 7-night caps; state-authorized local-option levies (Norway, Wales, Massachusetts).

relative_to_primary_file.overlap: None — these are destinations NOT in the primary file. Primary covered Venice/Amsterdam/Paris/Rome/etc.; this covers Hong Kong/Melbourne/India/Chicago/Baja Sur/Porto/etc.
